fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method provenance
Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor) for formaldehyde; headline value reported is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier “standard”), no density fitting (exact 4-center integrals) in PySCF v2.x backend.
Geometry provenance: single-point analysis on the user-supplied geometry at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz (no re-optimization; notation DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge and multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).  N–1 and N+1 states (cation and anion) were treated as doublets (charge = ±1, mult = 2) per default protocol.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`), PySCF backend.
Convergence & caveats: all three single-point SCFs (neutral, cation, anion) converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true.  Engine issued the following warnings verbatim:
• fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• schema_header: common header present
A live text log of the run was written during execution to the skill-generated `.out` file in the working directory (created automatically by chemkit) and can be tailed for full SCF details.  The structured result JSON is at /home/erobles/formaldehyde_fukui_b3lyp.json, and the bar-chart PNG is at the path given in the result JSON.

Key results (Mulliken-based indices, atoms ordered O(1) C(2) H(3) H(4))
f⁺  =  0.46813824, 0.70839130, –0.088269392, –0.088260145
f⁻  =  0.46742464, 0.11244422,  0.21007563,  0.21005551
f⁰  =  0.46778144, 0.41041776,  0.060903121, 0.060897683
Dual = 0.00071359555, 0.59594709, –0.29834503, –0.29831566

Interpretation
• Most electrophilic site: atom 2 (carbonyl carbon, C) with f⁺ = 0.70839130.
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (carbonyl oxygen, O) with f⁻ = 0.46742464.

These results were obtained with the chemkit fukui-reactivity workflow at the specified B3LYP/def2-TZVP gas-phase level of theory; provenance = computed.
